questions.question: Why should auth tokens be stored in cookies rather than localStorage in a Nuxt SSR app?
options: Cookies are more secure and immune to XSS attacks
localStorage doesn't exist on the server — cookies are sent with every HTTP request
localStorage is synchronous and would block the SSR render
Vue's useCookie composable is more performant than localStorage access

questions.question: Which Nuxt file initializes the user session on both server and client when the app starts?
Why must the auth cookie NOT be httpOnly when using Nuxt's useCookie composable to manage it client-side?
options: httpOnly cookies are not sent with same-site requests
httpOnly cookies cannot be accessed by JavaScript, so useCookie would read null
httpOnly prevents the SSR server from reading the cookie during hydration
Nuxt's $fetch ignores httpOnly cookies for security reasons
